rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical methoxysulfonylation/cyclization of N-methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide with NaHSO3 and MeOH to form a methylsulfonyl-functionalized isoquinolinedione
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bisulfite (HSO3-) to a sulfonyl radical (and/or oxidation of methanol-derived species), which adds to the methacrylamide alkene to trigger radical cyclization

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from sulfonyl radical addition to methacrylamide alkene
aryl/cyclohexadienyl radical after intramolecular ipso/ortho cyclization onto benzamide arene
methoxy-substituted alpha-aryl-beta-methylsulfonyl ethanol model compound (NMR D-labeling study)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes bisulfite to a sulfonyl radical that adds to the terminal alkene of the methacrylamide; the resulting tertiary C-radical undergoes intramolecular cyclization onto the pendant aryl ring, followed by rearomatization (further oxidation/loss of H+) with MeOH incorporation to give the methoxy/methylsulfonyl-functionalized isoquinolinedione product.

probe_reagents.name: deuterated MeOH (CD3OD / H/D labeling)
probe_reagents.observation: 1H NMR integrations on model methoxysulfonyl product show partial D incorporation (0.24D and 0.65D at OMe/SO2OMe positions), consistent with MeOH being the source of the methoxy group

probe_reagents.name: N-methacryloyl-N-methylbenzamide (radical cyclization substrate)
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis with MeOH/NaHSO3 gives the cyclized isoquinoline-1,3-dione bearing a CH2SO2OMe group (product 7, 18%), supporting a radical addition/cyclization pathway

notes: The radical-cyclization probe (substrate 6 → isoquinolinedione 7) functions as a radical clock, indicating a sulfonyl-radical addition followed by intramolecular aryl cyclization. H/D labeling on a model sulfonate ester suggests MeOH is the methoxy source. No TEMPO/BHT trapping or DFT was shown on this page.
